response_text:
# Git Branching Strategies: GitFlow vs Trunk-Based Development

## The Core Philosophy Difference

```
GitFlow: Multiple long-lived branches, structured releases
Trunk-Based: Everyone integrates to main frequently, short-lived branches
```

---

## GitFlow

### Branch Structure
```
main (production)
│
├── develop (integration branch)
│   │
│   ├── feature/user-auth        ← feature branches
│   ├── feature/payment-system
│   │
│   └── ── release/1.2.0         ← release branches
│           │
│           └── ── main          ← merge to main + tag
│
└── hotfix/critical-bug-fix      ← branches from main
```

### Branch Types & Lifecycle

```bash
# 1. FEATURE BRANCHES
# Branch from: develop
# Merge to: develop
git checkout develop
git checkout -b feature/user-authentication

# ... work for days/weeks ...

git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff feature/user-authentication
git branch -d feature/user-authentication


# 2. RELEASE BRANCHES
# Branch from: develop
# Merge to: main AND develop
git checkout develop
git checkout -b release/1.2.0

# Only bug fixes, docs, release prep here
git commit -m "Bump version to 1.2.0"
git commit -m "Fix last-minute release bug"

# When ready:
git checkout main
git merge --no-ff release/1.2.0
git tag -a v1.2.0 -m "Release version 1.2.0"

git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff release/1.2.0
git branch -d release/1.2.0


# 3. HOTFIX BRANCHES
# Branch from: main
# Merge to: main AND develop
git checkout main
git checkout -b hotfix/security-vulnerability

git commit -m "Fix SQL injection vulnerability"

git checkout main
git merge --no-ff hotfix/security-vulnerability
git tag -a v1.2.1

git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff hotfix/security-
